phase1.explanation:
try-catch-finally handles exceptions gracefully:

try block:
• Code that might throw an exception
• If exception occurs, jumps to catch

catch block:
• Handles specific exception types
• Multiple catches for different exceptions
• Order matters: specific → general (Exception last!)

finally block:
• ALWAYS executes (even if exception occurs)
• Used for cleanup (close files, release resources)
• Runs even if catch block throws exception

Rules:
1. try must have catch or finally (or both)
2. catch blocks ordered from specific to general
3. Exception as last catch catches everything

Common pattern:
• try { risky code }
• catch (SpecificException e) { handle it }
• catch (Exception e) { handle everything else }
• finally { cleanup }

Life without try-catch: program crashes with scary error messages!

With try-catch: program recovers gracefully!

phase2.errorMessage:
TryCatchExample.java:12: error: exception ArithmeticException has already been caught
        catch (ArithmeticException e) {  // Unreachable!
        ^
1 error
phase2.explanation:
You can't catch ArithmeticException after catching Exception!

Exception catches ALL exceptions (including ArithmeticException), so the ArithmeticException catch is unreachable.

FIX: Order catches from most specific to most general:

catch (ArithmeticException e) { ... }        // Specific first
catch (ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException e) { ... }
catch (Exception e) { ... }                  // Generic last

Always put the most specific exceptions first!

phase3.prompt:
Write a program that:
1. Creates a Scanner for user input
2. In try block: asks for two numbers and divides first by second
3. Catches ArithmeticException (division by zero)
4. Catches Exception (for any other error)
5. Has finally block that prints "Operation complete"

Test it with both valid and invalid inputs.
dialogueHints: Use try { risky code } catch (ArithmeticException e) { handle } catch (Exception e) { handle } finally { cleanup }
ArithmeticException catch should be BEFORE Exception catch
Finally block runs no matter what
